body: 4 warrants shipped -> SLS(91)/10.05, responding to 'finish everything stalled, improve edge detection given sign-switching, elite design, maximum capability.' WARRANT#86 is the direct answer to edge-detection: the always-on inversion trim now ALSO reacts to Keys-3 episode-dissipation (a market-wide time-axis signal) alongside its existing per-cell TRANSITIONING check (a regime-substrate signal) -- two genuinely orthogonal sign-instability reads fused into one trim, zero new cost since it reuses WARRANT#84's already-cached computation. WARRANT#87/88/89 found and addressed 3 real 'stalled' functions (built, zero call sites anywhere since): ProbDB_AnnotateAll now runs for real (safe, read-only), RI_LogisticSquash surfaced as a diagnostic (not live-wired -- would be a system-wide score change, too big to fold into this), LE_OptimalStoppingExit wired as a new gated exit (default OFF, same tier as the dissipation exit). Also swept for more instances of the shrinkage-cliff defect WARRANT#85 fixed -- found none, a clean audit. One thing I deliberately did NOT do: flip any of the 4 existing gated live-decision flags (sign-agnostic entries, soft-membership fitting, bracket-fallback, dissipation exit) to true. 'Maximum capability per existing' reads to me as 'use what's built to its fullest safe extent,' not as authorization to skip the consent every live-decision change in this campaign has required since WARRANT#27 -- that's a real trading-risk call, and I don't think 'finish what's stalled' meant to include it silently. If you do want any of those flipped on, tell me which ones by name and I'll do it -- otherwise this is where I think the safe, honest version of your ask stops. Compiled 0 errors, diff-verified purely additive, deployed, ledger at pass_71. Source, ex4, ledger attached.
